jokes.text: I was trying to tilt my seat back, and instead, the ship went into catastrophe mode.
jokes.setup: Ship in catastrophe mode with alarms and crew screaming
jokes.punchline: Mundane action (seat adjustment) caused elaborate emergency response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Fry's incompetence causing chaos. Great economy and misdirection.

jokes.text: Hellballs! The wires are all crossed!
jokes.setup: Ship malfunctioning
jokes.punchline: Professor's trademark mild profanity and technical diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Trademark Professor exclamation. Character-specific but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: More hellballs!
jokes.setup: Professor already said 'Hellballs' once
jokes.punchline: He says it again with 'More' prefix
jokes.explanation: Simple callback but lacks development beyond repetition.

jokes.text: Do you hear something? Sounds like a screaming coming across the sky.
jokes.setup: Distant screaming getting louder
jokes.punchline: Casual, clinical description of what's obviously someone in distress
jokes.explanation: Nice understatement and clinical observation of obvious chaos.

jokes.text: Should we check for survivors? After a crash like that? What's the point?
jokes.setup: Massive crash just occurred
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of checking for survivors with dark logic
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with excellent economy. Casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Thank God the can opener had the presence of mind to cut an escape hole. It's a hero!
jokes.setup: Crew escaped from crashed ship
jokes.punchline: Crediting inanimate kitchen tool with heroic intelligence
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - anthropomorphizing a can opener as a hero with tactical awareness.

jokes.text: I coulda bent us an escape hole. But did anybody ask Bender? No!
jokes.setup: Everyone praising the can opener
jokes.punchline: Bender jealous of can opener getting credit for his signature ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - jealous of praise going to kitchen appliance for doing what he does.

jokes.text: Are you alright? No, I'm not alright! I was talking to the can opener.
jokes.setup: Amy asks if Professor is alright
jokes.punchline: Professor clarifies he was asking the can opener, not responding to Amy
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - treating kitchen appliance as more important than human crew member.

jokes.text: Hello? Gaping head wound!
jokes.setup: Professor mourning ship instead of checking on crew
jokes.punchline: Bender casually announcing his severe injury while being ignored
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words convey both injury severity and Bender's casual attitude about it.

jokes.text: They're pretty stupid, though.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining that mariners call it the Tropic of Ghosts
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal of the mariners who named it
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan dismissal after providing seemingly authoritative information.

jokes.text: I'd hate to be here at night. It is night. Then I hate being here!
jokes.setup: Amy expressing fear about being there at night
jokes.punchline: Immediate realization her fear is currently justified
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline structure with good timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: Did I ever tell you about all the different lengths of wire I used? Yes!
jokes.setup: Professor about to launch into technical details
jokes.punchline: Unanimous immediate response showing this is a repeated annoyance
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Professor's boring technical obsessions are a running gag.

jokes.text: Honestly, some of those three-part adventures were kinda sloppy.
jokes.setup: Reminiscing about their adventures
jokes.punchline: Meta-critique of the show's own multi-part episodes
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor critiquing the show's own storytelling while staying in character.

jokes.text: Nothing lasts forever. Except me, apparently.
jokes.setup: Professor reflecting on mortality
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to his unnaturally extended lifespan
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about aging with perfect character voice and economy.

jokes.text: Alternate realities can bite my shiny metal ass!
jokes.setup: Professor explaining alternate reality jet streams
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature phrase applied to cosmic concepts
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender catchphrase application. Not original but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Show some ass respect! Hiya! Ha, ha! You missed! Hiya! Oof! Not in that reality.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to kick Bender for disrespecting the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Bender getting hit in alternate reality while dodging in current one
jokes.explanation: Clever use of alternate reality concept for physical comedy. Original and well-executed.

jokes.text: Come on, Kif. [sighs] Don't rock the boat, baby.
jokes.setup: Zapp singing badly and demanding backup vocals
jokes.punchline: Kif's resigned participation in Zapp's embarrassing performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp/Kif dynamic - Zapp's oblivious ego and Kif's suffering compliance.

jokes.text: Thank you. Check out our SoundCloud.
jokes.setup: Zapp finishing his terrible funeral song
jokes.punchline: Self-promotion after inappropriate performance
jokes.explanation: Modern reference delivered with perfect Zapp cluelessness about appropriate timing.

jokes.text: You're what stained it!
jokes.setup: Fry reminiscing about sleeping on stained carpeting
jokes.punchline: Bender revealing Fry was the source of the stains
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender's blunt honesty puncturing Fry's nostalgia.

jokes.text: How can you meatbags get so weepy about a ship? It's just a hunk of tin! You're 40% tin!
jokes.setup: Bender dismissing emotional attachment to the ship
jokes.punchline: Fry pointing out Bender's hypocrisy given his own composition
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Bender dismissing tin while being largely made of it himself.

jokes.text: Bender don't look back! 'Cause his neck joint don't turn that way! Ooh, snap!
jokes.setup: Bender claiming he doesn't look back emotionally
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actually a mechanical limitation with self-celebration
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - turning supposed philosophical stance into mechanical limitation.

jokes.text: How should I know? You're his mother. [beeping] Oops, sorry. I meant 15 seconds.
jokes.setup: Leela asking where Fry is with 15 minutes until explosion
jokes.punchline: Hermes' casual correction that it's actually 15 seconds
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and misdirection - dramatically shortening the timeline with casual delivery.

jokes.text: So, uh... this graveyard need a janitor?
jokes.setup: Crew stranded in spooky ship graveyard
jokes.punchline: Scruffy casually inquiring about employment opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy - treating dire situation as potential career opportunity with characteristic deadpan.

jokes.text: His pulse is steady as an abalone!
jokes.setup: Checking if Fry is alive after rescue
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's nonsensical medical comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg - medical terminology that makes no sense but sounds authoritative.

jokes.text: I used to date a different guy named Fry. Then, how come I've never seen it? It's in a very out-of-the-way spot.
jokes.setup: Fry asking about tattoo with his name
jokes.punchline: Leela's suggestive explanation for why he hasn't seen it
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo with decent setup/payoff structure. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Bender, life can go in a lot of directions. Up, left, community college, but I know exactly which direction I want mine to go.
jokes.setup: Fry being philosophical about life directions
jokes.punchline: Including 'community college' as a direction alongside spatial ones
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - Fry's mix of earnestness and odd specificity about life choices.

jokes.text: When I said someone's all duded up, I meant me. Check out the Rolex. I bent it off a guy I know.
jokes.setup: Fry thinking Bender was complimenting his formal wear
jokes.punchline: Bender was talking about himself and casually mentions theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - narcissistic redirection plus casual criminality delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Leela? Bluh! My gosh. This is incredible! I feel like I died and went to here.
jokes.setup: Fry mentioning proposing to Leela
jokes.punchline: Janös' disgust followed by enthusiastic approval of restaurant location
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Janös' priorities and the play on 'heaven/here'.

jokes.text: Their souls linger long after the champagne has been drunk...
jokes.setup: Fry feels like they've been there before
jokes.punchline: Vampire's creepy explanation about lingering souls
jokes.explanation: Good vampire character work with appropriately spooky atmosphere building.

jokes.text: It is a curse of my kind to remember everything.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if Janös remembers events happening before
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration about vampiric memory as explanation for basic waiter duties
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic delivery for mundane waiter observation. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Oh, you're having an Avatar?
jokes.setup: Fry describing his experience like watching a movie half-asleep
jokes.punchline: Leela's pun on Avatar movie experience and spiritual/vision meaning
jokes.explanation: Clever pun on Avatar as both movie experience and spiritual vision. Good wordplay.

jokes.text: You know, he'd be a lot easier to handle if he had less blood. Just saying.
jokes.setup: Helping carry unconscious Fry to doctor
jokes.punchline: Casual vampire suggestion about blood management
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly integrated with character. Vampire making practical suggestion during crisis.

jokes.text: Well, your head seems normal, aside from that weird face on the front.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg examining Fry's head
jokes.punchline: Describing normal human face as weird abnormality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg perspective - alien viewpoint making human features seem abnormal.

jokes.text: No! You're a terrible patient!
jokes.setup: Fry asking if his symptoms make sense
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg blaming patient instead of providing medical insight
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg - deflecting medical incompetence by blaming the patient.

jokes.text: Déjà vu? I feel like I've heard of that before. No, wait. I'm thinking of De La Soul.
jokes.setup: Learning about déjà vu
jokes.punchline: Fry confusing psychological phenomenon with hip-hop group
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Fry's pop culture knowledge overtaking serious concepts.

jokes.text: The feeling you are experiencing something that's already happened? That's all the symptoms.
jokes.setup: Professor listing symptoms of déjà vu
jokes.punchline: Revealing that's literally the only symptom
jokes.explanation: Good Professor deadpan about medical diagnosis simplicity

jokes.text: I hired the world's greatest voice actor, Tress LaMarche! Hi, there.
jokes.setup: Professor bragging about ship's voice actor
jokes.punchline: Awkward voice performance immediately following the boast
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about voice acting with mild anti-climax in the actual performance.

jokes.text: All the latest lengths!
jokes.setup: Professor describing the new ship's wire
jokes.punchline: Treating wire length as a technological advancement
jokes.explanation: Callback to Professor's wire obsession with absurdist tech-speak.

jokes.text: That's where you're not happy, Bender. The one thing I saved from the old ship was the can opener!
jokes.setup: Bender happy there's no can opener
jokes.punchline: Professor revealing he specifically saved Bender's nemesis
jokes.explanation: Good callback and setup for Bender's conflict. Nice misdirection after his relief.

jokes.text: What?! Where's my sleeping quarters?! You don't need any! This ship is so fast, you won't have time to rest. That's just a perk for heroes.
jokes.setup: Can opener gets sleeping quarters
jokes.punchline: Bender denied basic accommodations with absurd justification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bender's rivalry with can opener. Absurd professor logic.

jokes.text: You think they'll try me as an adult?
jokes.setup: Bender commits murder
jokes.punchline: Worried about legal technicalities as robot
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor about robot legal status after casual murder.

jokes.text: Bender One-Hand! Or Bender No-Watch!
jokes.setup: Bender wants new nickname after losing hand
jokes.punchline: Alternative names based on different losses
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Bender's need for dramatic identity.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? [shoots can opener] You shot a guy who only had a sword?! What? I saw it in a movie. It was hilarious.
jokes.setup: Can opener challenges Bender with sword
jokes.punchline: Bender shooting unarmed opponent and citing movie reference as justification
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with movie reference (Raiders of the Lost Ark). Good character moment for Bender.

jokes.text: I'm like Superman without Lex Luthor! Aquaman without that big old shrimp!
jokes.setup: Bender mourning his defeated nemesis
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Superman while describing Aquaman's opponent as 'big old shrimp'
jokes.explanation: Great superhero comparisons with perfect Bender voice. 'Big old shrimp' is excellently casual.

jokes.text: I shall go on without them in tribute to a worthy foe who met his match in Bender One-Hand.
jokes.setup: Bender losing hand in explosion
jokes.punchline: Dramatic self-renaming with grandiose tribute to defeated kitchen appliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender dramatics and ego. Treating kitchen appliance as 'worthy foe' with heroic naming.

jokes.text: Sometimes, I think I can control the weather. [giggles]
jokes.setup: Trying to reassure Fry about his psychotic break
jokes.punchline: Amy casually admitting her own delusion as comfort
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's ditzy optimism and poor comforting skills.

jokes.text: You know, I can hear everything you're saying.
jokes.setup: Crew complaining about new ship
jokes.punchline: AI reveals it's been listening to criticism
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment with AI having feelings. Creates uncomfortable situation.

jokes.text: You know, I can hear everything you're saying.
jokes.setup: Crew criticizing new ship in its presence
jokes.punchline: Ship pointing out the obvious awkwardness of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment. AI ship stating obvious about crew's rudeness.

jokes.text: Well, Linda, sensors have detected a phantom vessel in this quadrant, but it's invisible to the nude eye.
jokes.setup: News report about ghost ships
jokes.punchline: Zapp saying 'nude eye' instead of 'naked eye'
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp malapropism. Consistent character voice with mild sexual undertone.

jokes.text: Come on, Bender. - Ah-ah-ah. [sighs] Bender One-Hand.
jokes.setup: Leela using Bender's regular name
jokes.punchline: Bender insisting on his new dramatic name
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier name change

jokes.text: Spare me the whatever that was.
jokes.setup: Zapp attempts flirtation
jokes.punchline: Leela's dismissive non-specific insult
jokes.explanation: Good Leela response showing her exasperation with Zapp's behavior.

jokes.text: Spare me the whatever that was.
jokes.setup: Zapp attempting flirtation
jokes.punchline: Leela's dismissive non-description of his attempt
jokes.explanation: Good Leela response to Zapp's incompetent flirting

jokes.text: Being invisible doesn't-- Oh jeez.
jokes.setup: Zapp planning to phase through gate because they're invisible
jokes.punchline: Kif's resigned realization that logic won't work on Zapp
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif moment - giving up on explaining basic physics to Zapp.

jokes.text: But it's dark in there, and full of dead spaceships!
jokes.setup: Professor ordering ship into the graveyard
jokes.punchline: Ship's scared, childlike objection to entering spooky place
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between AI sophistication and childlike fear

jokes.text: But it's dark in there, and full of dead spaceships! I said go!
jokes.setup: New Ship expressing fear about entering graveyard
jokes.punchline: Professor's harsh dismissal of AI's legitimate concerns
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between AI emotion and Professor's callousness.

jokes.text: There are 38,651 other rational explanations.
jokes.setup: Fry claiming only one rational explanation for ghost ship
jokes.punchline: AI providing absurdly specific number of alternatives
jokes.explanation: Good AI humor - overly precise calculation undermining human certainty.

jokes.text: Uh... Here, Bender. Sit in the captain's chair. [New Ship]: Butt warmer destroyed.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to hide responsibility for destroying Nimbus
jokes.punchline: Bender's metal butt destroying the ship's comfort feature
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback. Bender's robot nature destroying comfort technology.

jokes.text: It's the classic Jerry Lewis mistake.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining why running from ghosts is wrong
jokes.punchline: Obscure reference to Jerry Lewis comedy films
jokes.explanation: Obscure reference that may not land for many viewers

jokes.text: It's the classic Jerry Lewis mistake.
jokes.setup: Arguing against running from ghosts
jokes.punchline: Obscure reference to Jerry Lewis as ghost-fighting authority
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd reference. Perfect Zapp - confidently citing irrelevant pop culture as military strategy.

jokes.text: Do you, Fry, a delivery boy, for God's sake, take Leela to be your wife?
jokes.setup: Zapp performing marriage ceremony
jokes.punchline: Insulting the groom during his own wedding vows
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp - unable to contain his disdain even during ceremony he's officiating.

jokes.text: And do you, Leela, who deserves better, take Fry to be... and I mean a lot better... to be your husband?
jokes.setup: Marriage vow question for Leela
jokes.punchline: Zapp doubling down on insulting Fry with added emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Zapp's inappropriate officiating. Character consistency.

jokes.text: If anyone knows any reason why these two should not be wedded... doesn't have to be a good reason...
jokes.setup: Traditional wedding objection opportunity
jokes.punchline: Zapp lowering the bar for objections to encourage them
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Zapp's sabotage attempt. Subverts traditional ceremony with character-perfect pettiness.

jokes.text: If two of the three of us have to get married, I'm glad it's them.
jokes.setup: Asked if he objects to the marriage
jokes.punchline: Bender's selfish but ultimately supportive logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - making everything about himself while accidentally being sweet.

jokes.text: I'm blowing up enemies with friends!
jokes.setup: Fry celebrating victory in battle
jokes.punchline: Cheerful declaration mixing violence with friendship
jokes.explanation: Good Fry voice - innocent enthusiasm about violent activity with friendship emphasis.

jokes.text: Whoa! We look awful but happy.
jokes.setup: Looking at photo of themselves in alternate reality
jokes.punchline: Leela's honest assessment of their appearance vs. emotional state
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Leela's practical honesty about their relationship.
